title: Mild Korean Smoked Mackerel Namul
description: A herbal and pillowy Korean dish featuring smoked mackerel, yuzu cure, and low fat friendly ingredients, originating from the Gangwon region, typically served as a side dish
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together yuzu juice, soy sauce, garlic, and ginger until well combined.
Add the smoked mackerel to the bowl and gently fold until the mackerel is fully coated with the yuzu mixture.
Heat the sesame oil in a small pan over medium heat.
Add the chopped green onions to the pan and cook until they are slightly softened, about 2 minutes.
Remove the pan from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Add the cooked green onions, toasted sesame seeds, salt, and black pepper to the bowl with the mackerel mixture.
Gently fold all the ingredients together until they are well combined.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Serve the namul chilled, garnished with additional toasted sesame seeds and green onions if desired.
